questions.question: What does your claims fraud detection process look like today — manual review, rules-based, or analytics-driven?
questions.intent: Baseline maturity check. Most PH insurers still rely on manual adjuster review with basic red-flag rules.
questions.followUp: What percentage of claims go through SIU investigation?

questions.question: How are you governing your pricing and underwriting models — is there a centralized model inventory with version control?
questions.intent: Insurance Commission increasingly scrutinizing model use in pricing. Opens model governance conversation.
questions.followUp: How do you document model assumptions for IC examination?

questions.question: Where are you in your IFRS 17 implementation — are your actuarial calculations still spreadsheet-based or have you automated?
questions.intent: IFRS 17 is a massive PH insurance pain point. Automation = SAS opportunity.
questions.followUp: How long does your quarterly close process take for insurance contracts?

questions.question: Have you seen PhilHealth-related fraud patterns in your claims — inflated hospital bills, ghost patients, or provider collusion?
questions.intent: PhilHealth fraud is endemic. SAS network analytics can detect provider rings.
questions.followUp: How do you currently validate provider billing against PhilHealth case rates?

questions.question: How automated is your underwriting process — can you score a standard policy application in real-time?
questions.intent: Straight-through processing is a competitive differentiator. SAS decisioning enables this.
questions.followUp: What's the average turnaround time for a policy application today?

questions.question: Are you leveraging telematics or IoT data for motor insurance pricing — or is it still traditional rating factors?
questions.intent: Usage-based insurance is emerging in PH. SAS can ingest and score IoT data streams.
questions.followUp: What data sources feed your motor pricing models today?

questions.question: How do you identify cross-sell and up-sell opportunities across your policyholder base — is it analytics-driven or campaign-based?
questions.intent: Customer lifetime value and next-best-offer are SAS sweet spots.

questions.question: How do you track model performance in production — do you have automated drift detection or is it periodic manual review?
questions.intent: Model degradation in insurance is costly. Claims fraud models need continuous monitoring.
questions.followUp: What happens when a model underperforms — how quickly can you retrain and redeploy?
